2007-09-01.title: Resurgence of the Taliban
2007-09-01.abstract: Despite NATO's continued efforts, Taliban fighters have returned to roughly half of a strategic area they occupied in southern Afghanistan last year.

2007-09-01.title: Agreeing to Block Some Videos, YouTube Returns to Thailand
2007-09-01.abstract: Thai censors lifted their ban on YouTube after five months of blocking it because it had carried material seen as insulting to the country’s king.

2007-09-01.title: Freeze on Wages Is Latest Step to Stanch Inflation in Zimbabwe
2007-09-01.abstract: The new freeze, announced on Friday, bars businesses from indexing wages or fees to inflation, a method employed in many wage agreements.

2007-09-01.title: Iraqi Calls for Broader Cease-Fire
2007-09-01.abstract: The Iraqi government called on all armed groups to join a cease-fire declared this week by Moktada al-Sadr.

2007-09-01.title: Political Ally Says Musharraf Will Quit Army Post
2007-09-01.abstract: The Pakistani official said the plan was not related to any deal with the former prime minister, Benazir Bhutto.

2007-09-02.title: Freed by Taliban, 19 South Korean Hostages Will Face Relief and Anger Back Home
2007-09-02.abstract: Many in South Korea are growing increasingly angry at the decision of the Christian volunteers to travel to South Korea despite government warnings.

2007-09-02.title: Hamas Forces Shoot Own Supporters at Rally; Youth Killed

2007-09-02.abstract: Two announcements today appeared intended to enforce Iran’s refusal to back down in the face of stepped-up pressure from the United States.

2007-09-02.title: West Bank Boys Dig a Living in Settler Trash
2007-09-02.abstract: In a sign of the dire economic conditions for Palestinians, a dump is now a lifeline for scavengers.

2007-09-02.abstract: The move will intensify economic assistance to Sunni groups, particularly in Anbar Province, who have turned against homegrown insurgent groups.

2007-09-03.title: Cleric Issues Warning on Karbala Investigation
2007-09-03.abstract: Moktada al-Sadr demanded that an investigation into gun battles in the holy city of Karbala, Iraq, be impartial.

2007-09-03.abstract: Supporters of the privately-run American University of Iraq hop that it will stand as a symbol of positive change that is possible at least in Iraq’s Kurdish north.

2007-09-03.title: Arab Tribes Battling in Darfur Over Spoils of War
2007-09-03.abstract: Some of the Arab tribes accused of massacring civilians in Darfur are now unleashing their firepower against each other in a battle that is displacing tens of thousands.

2007-09-03.title: Ethiopian Rebels to Refrain From Attacks
2007-09-03.abstract: The temporary cease-fire is intended to allow a United Nations fact-finding team to gain access to the war-torn region.

2007-09-03.title: British Troops Begin Withdrawal From Basra
2007-09-03.abstract: The move will leave the southern Iraqi city without foreign forces for the first time since the American-led invasion in 2003.

2007-09-04.abstract: The last four years of bloodshed have been described as a conflict between Arab and non-Arab tribes.  Now, some Arab tribes are fighting each other.

2007-09-04.title: Congo's Treacherous Trains
2007-09-04.abstract: Congo is attempting to get its railway infrastructure, over 100 years old and neglected during years of civil war, running again.

2007-09-04.title: Coordinated Blasts Kill at Least 25 in Pakistan
2007-09-04.abstract: The bombings near the headquarters of the military in Rawalpindi come at a time of great political tension.

2007-09-04.title: Vatican City: Policeman Kills Himself
2007-09-04.abstract: A 25-year-old Vatican gendarme was found dead of a gunshot wound in a barracks bathroom, apparently a suicide, the Vatican said.

2007-09-04.title: Rocket Fired From Gaza Lands Near Israeli School
2007-09-04.abstract: A rocket fired from Gaza landed in an empty courtyard between a day care center and an elementary school in the Israeli border town of Sderot.

2007-09-05.title: Death Sentence Upheld for Hussein Henchman
2007-09-05.abstract: The decision paved the way for the henchman, Ali Hassan al-Majid, a cousin of Saddam Hussein, to be executed within 30 days in accordance with Iraqi law.

2007-09-05.title: Eight Arrests in Bomb Plot in Denmark
2007-09-05.abstract: Investigators described the threat as imminent and said the arrests were the result of an international investigation that had lasted several months.

2007-09-05.title: Battle Over Iraq Strategy
2007-09-05.abstract: As the nation awaits next week’s report on the status of the Iraq war, the battle for the hearts and minds of Americans is escalating on the airwaves.

2007-09-05.title: Suicide Blasts Kill 25 in Pakistani Garrison City
2007-09-05.abstract: Powerful coordinated explosions were set off by two suicide bombers in Rawalpindi, a garrison city near the capital.

2007-09-05.title: Democrats Aim to Reframe Iraq Debate
2007-09-05.abstract: As Congress reopened for business on Tuesday, the Democratic leadership promised to force a change in President Bush’s war strategy.

2007-09-06.title: An Everyday Substance, Potent When Concentrated
2007-09-06.abstract: Hydrogen peroxide, the chemical that the German police say two terrorism suspects planned to use to make bombs, is a common substance in diluted form, but at greater concentrations can be used to make bombs.

2007-09-06.abstract: Ban Ki-moon received a rousing welcome at a camp, Al Salam, but was jeered outside by supporters of Sudanese President Omar Hassan al-Bashir.

2007-09-06.title: New Tsunami Warning
2007-09-06.abstract: An undersea fault line near the crowded river deltas of the northern Bay of Bengal is capable of unleashing a potent tsunami, although there is no indication that such a threat is imminent.

2007-09-06.title: Democrats Newly Willing to Compromise on Iraq
2007-09-06.abstract: Senate Democrats are trying to win Republican support for forcing at least modest troop withdrawals.

2007-09-06.abstract: In hearings  before Congress on Wednesday, a senior official described problems with staffing and coordination at the agencies in charge of reconstruction projects in Iraq.

2007-09-07.title: Muted Expectations as Benedict Heads to Austria
2007-09-07.abstract: The three-day visit to Austria highlights a central — and difficult — question of Benedict’s papacy: Which believers, exactly, does this pope talk to?

2007-09-07.title: Jobs Abroad Support ‘Model’ State in India
2007-09-07.abstract: The Indian state of Kerala, celebrated for its alternative model of development, is dependent on remittances.

2007-09-07.title: 2 Recent Storms Show Forests Help Blunt Hurricanes’ Force
2007-09-07.abstract: Authorities are crediting heavily forested areas along the Caribbean coast with sapping the energy of Hurricane Felix when it made landfall.

2007-09-07.abstract: Interior Minister Michèle Alliot-Marie announced the creation of a special police unit for gang violence, in an attempt to halt a rise in clashes between youth gangs in the Paris region.

2007-09-07.abstract: Gen. David H. Petraeus wants to keep heightened troop levels in Iraq well into next year, but could accept the pullback of one brigade in January, officials say.

2007-09-08.title: Nuclear Experts to Inspect Sites in North Korea
2007-09-08.abstract: At the invitation of North Korea, an international delegation of nuclear experts will travel to the North next week to inspect nuclear sites that are to be shut down.

2007-09-08.title: Bin Laden Releases Video as C.I.A. Issues Warning
2007-09-08.abstract: Details of a new video emerged as the director of the C.I.A. gave a public warning about Al Qaeda’s growing strength.

2007-09-08.title: Pope Vigorously Defends Catholicism in Austria and Raises Concerns on Europe’s Future
2007-09-08.abstract: Pope Benedict XVI warned that Europe may extinguish itself if it embraces abortion and rejects Christianity.

2007-09-08.title: Gaza Under Hamas: Quiet, Cut Off and Digging In
2007-09-08.abstract: Nearly three months after Hamas took control of Gaza, a wary calm has taken hold in the increasingly isolated territory.

2007-09-09.title: Blast Kills 15 in Shiite Area of Baghdad
2007-09-09.abstract: The bombing was the first against Shiites in Baghdad since last week, when the cleric Moktada al-Sadr ordered his militia to halt all attacks.

2007-09-09.title: Afghan Suicide Attacks Rising, Report Shows

2007-09-09.abstract: Secretary General Ban Ki-moon met with Col. Muammar el-Qaddafi and said the Libyan leader had pledged to use his influence on behalf of a peace conference next month.

2007-09-09.abstract: Luciano Pavarotti, the Italian tenor, was eulogized on Saturday as a “great artist” with “a profound sense of humanity.”

2007-09-09.abstract: Amid jazz clubs and seaside strolls, leaders of Colombia’s E.L.N. rebel group take refuge in Havana, the birthplace of their movement, while they negotiate an end to their insurgency.

2007-09-10.title: Iranian Raises Possibility of an Intrusion Into Iraq
2007-09-10.abstract: Iranian diplomats suggested that Iran might respond militarily if the Iraqi government failed to prevent militants from crossing in to Iran to carry out attacks.

2007-09-10.title: Still Married, With Children, but in Russian
2007-09-10.abstract: Despite the Kremlin’s drumbeat of anti-Americanism, Russians have embraced imports of American sitcoms.

2007-09-10.title: Costumes Malfunction, but Never the Guitars
2007-09-10.abstract: At the Air Guitar World Championships in Oulu, Finland, striking a chord is, at best, metaphorical.

2007-09-10.title: Ex-Premier of Pakistan Arrested Upon His Return
2007-09-10.abstract: Nawaz Sharif, a Pakistani opposition leader and former prime minister, was flown to Saudi Arabia after his arrest in Islamabad.

2007-09-11.title: Sharif Appeals Deportation
2007-09-11.abstract: Lawyers for the former Pakistani prime minister today threw down a legal challenge to  Gen. Pervez Musharraf.

2007-09-11.abstract: Gen. David H. Petraeus said that by next summer the U.S. should be able to reduce its troop strength in Iraq to the level it was at before the recent increase.

2007-09-11.abstract: United Nations officials said they did not know if Ebola was the cause of a mystery illness that has killed more than 100 people.

2007-09-12.title: Baghdad Reaction
2007-09-12.abstract: Alissa J. Rubin reports on the ambivalent reaction Iraqis had to the progress report presented to Congress.

2007-09-12.abstract: Among the 45 senators  who questioned Gen. David H. Petraeus this week, five are running for president.

2007-09-12.abstract: The European Union has reversed a plan that would have required Britain and Ireland to phase out imperial measurements in favor of the metric system.

2007-09-12.abstract: A Philippine anticorruption court convicted Joseph Estrada of illegally acquiring wealth while in office and sentenced him to a maximum of 40 years in prison.

2007-09-12.abstract: The lethal Ebola virus has resurfaced in central Congo, U.N.  officials said, and health experts were rushing supplies and doctors into the remote area to contain the disease.

2007-09-13.abstract: The nomination of a low-profile cabinet official to be prime minister raised questions about who might succeed President Vladimir V. Putin if he steps down.

2007-09-17.abstract: Iraqis fleeing their homes are leaving behind uncrossable neighborhood lines and a shattered sense of trust.

2007-09-18.abstract: Gen. Pervez Musharraf plans to run for another term but resign his military post if he is re-elected as president.

2007-09-18.abstract: The largest American project in Afghanistan is the repair and upgrade of the Kajaki Dam, which lies in the most problematic of all of Afghanistan’s provinces.

2007-09-20.abstract: The election has become the focus of the deepest political crisis General Musharraf has faced.

2007-09-20.abstract: The Alliance for a Green Revolution in Africa said it would develop a center to train African scientists to develop hardier, higher-yielding varieties of crops.

2007-09-20.abstract: The involvement of the Buddhist clergy, which is revered in this country, presents a serious challenge to the junta.

2007-09-21.abstract: In the continuing debate over the origin of the extinct “little people” of Indonesia, a team of scientists says it has found evidence that these people were members of a distinct species.

2007-09-21.abstract: Chile’s Supreme Court is expected to announce on Friday its response to Peru’s request to extradite Alberto Fujimori, who faces charges of corruption and human rights abuses

2007-09-21.abstract: Democrats and Republicans in the Senate continued their circular debate over the Iraq war on Thursday, with the same arguments and the same voting results.

2007-09-21.abstract: About 40 Taliban fighters and at least four civilians have been killed in fierce clashes in southern Afghanistan, officials said today, while two NATO soldiers were killed in other attacks.

2007-09-22.abstract: American and Iraqi forces still face problems protecting residents in 16 percent of Baghdad’s neighborhoods.

2007-09-22.abstract: The move was an apparent shift for the Vatican, which has long struggled with the Chinese government over the authority to appoint bishops in China.

2007-09-22.abstract: The U.S. and France agree on stepping up economic and diplomatic pressure on Iran over its nuclear program, Secretary of State Condoleezza Rice said Friday.

2007-09-22.abstract: Israeli military officials said the leader of a joint cell of Hamas and the Popular Front for the Liberation of Palestine was preparing a suicide bombing in Israel during the religious holiday.

2007-09-23.abstract: A Berlin neighborhood known for its baby boom reveals that in a nation with a graying population, treasure hunts often end in disappointment.

2007-09-23.abstract: The presidential election is unlikely to occur before next month, as a political stalemate continues.

2007-09-23.title: Moderate Is Chosen as Japan’s Next Prime Minister
2007-09-23.abstract: Yasuo Fukuda, a mild-mannered political moderate, is known for his ability to build consensus behind the scenes.

2007-09-23.abstract: The drive by a revitalized Russian Orthodox Church to weave its tenets into the education system has prompted a backlash.

2007-09-24.abstract: The arrest of a Pakistani-born German citizen offers glimpses of the traffic into the tribal areas of Pakistan and the attempt by the authorities to monitor it.

2007-09-24.abstract: Prime Minister Nuri Kamal al-Maliki said his government was working with the U.S. to bring those responsible in last week’s shooting to justice.

2007-09-24.abstract: Diplomats from 18 countries expressed concern about the rise of opium poppy cultivation in Afghanistan to President Hamid Karzai.

2007-09-24.abstract: Army snipers have begun planting fake weaponry and killing anyone who picks it up, according to testimony.

2007-09-25.abstract: President Gloria Macapagal-Arroyo has made it known through her advisers that she is considering a pardon for her predecessor, Joseph Estrada, who was recently convicted of plunder.

2007-09-25.abstract: Nuri Kamal al-Maliki, the prime minister of Iraq, attended a traditional Ramadan feast at a Queens mosque Monday night.

2007-09-25.title: Russia: New Ministers
2007-09-25.abstract: President Vladimir V. Putin announced the formation of his new government, nearly two weeks after surprising the country by appointing a relative unknown, Viktor A. Zubkov, as his prime minister. Mr. Putin made only three changes to his old cabinet, replacing his economics minister, German O. Gref, with Mr. Gref’s deputy, Elvira S. Nabiullina, and announcing new health and regional development ministers. Mr. Putin also rejected the resignation of Defense Minister Anatoly E. Serdyukov, who had said he was stepping down because he wanted to avoid the appearance of nepotism. Mr. Serdyukov is married to Mr. Zubkov’s daughter.

2007-09-26.title: Iraqi Premier Breaks Fast Queens
2007-09-26.abstract: Nuri Kamal al-Maliki, the prime minister of Iraq, attended a traditional Ramadan feast at a Queens mosque.

2007-09-26.abstract: The Democratic chairman of a House committee complained Tuesday that the State Department was blocking his panel’s efforts to investigate the private security firm Blackwater USA and its operations in Iraq.

2007-09-26.abstract: The shadowy Marxist rebel group that has rattled Mexico in recent months by bombing natural gas pipelines has a long history of financing its operations with kidnappings.

2007-09-26.abstract: The National Art Gallery in Islamabad, Pakistan, which opened last month, has brought new texture to an otherwise sterile, highly planned capital.

2007-09-26.abstract: Mahmoud Ahmadinejad said that he considered the dispute over his country’s nuclear program “closed.”

2007-09-27.abstract: Blackwater's aggressive tactics have faced growing criticism from both the U.S. and Iraqi governments.

2007-09-27.abstract: Blackwater's aggressive tactics have faced growing criticism from both the U.S. and Iraqi governments.

2007-09-27.title: Canada: Endangered Shellfish Imports
2007-09-27.abstract: Canadian wildlife officials have charged two companies and three men with illegally importing large amounts of Queen conch shellfish, an endangered species, before exporting some of it to the United States.

2007-09-27.title: China Blocks Move to Condemn Crackdown
2007-09-27.abstract: China blocked an effort on Wednesday to have the Security Council condemn Myanmar’s violent crackdown on protesters.

2007-09-27.title: Red Cross Workers Kidnapped in Afghanistan
2007-09-27.abstract: Four employees who had traveled outside of the capital to help negotiate the release of hostages held by the Taliban were themselves kidnapped, the authorities said today.

2007-09-28.title: Campaigning as Obama's Cousin
2007-09-28.abstract: Kenyan parliamentary candidate Nicholas Owino Rajula claims he is a distant cousin of U.S. Senator Barack Obama, and uses that connection on the campaign trail.

2007-09-28.title: Health Fund Falls Well Short of Pledge Goal
2007-09-28.abstract: Donors pledged $9.7 billion to the Global Fund to Fight AIDS, Tuberculosis and Malaria at a fund-raising conference in Berlin, well short of the $15 billion to $18 billion the fund had hoped to raise.

2007-09-28.title: Beneath Booming Cities, China’s Future Is Drying Up
2007-09-28.abstract: Groundwater levels are dropping around China, where leaders face tough choices as cities, industry and farming compete for an unbalanced and finite water supply.

2007-09-28.title: Security Council Delays Vote on Iran Sanctions
2007-09-28.abstract: Six major world powers agreed today to delay until November a United Nations Security Council vote on a third set of sanctions against Iran.

2007-09-28.title: At Least Seven Reported Dead After U.S. Helicopter Attack
2007-09-28.abstract: Witnesses say the attack was launched by an American military helicopter in a suburb outside Baghdad.

2007-09-29.title: A Consumer Alerts the World
2007-09-29.abstract: How a Panamanian man's discovery triggered a global hunt for tainted toothpaste manufactured in China.

2007-09-29.title: Myanmar’s Descent, Seen From 150 Miles Up
2007-09-29.abstract: Satellite photographs of rural Myanmar show what seems to be evidence of human rights abuses gathered from space.

2007-09-29.title: At Climate Meeting, Bush Does Not Specify Goals
2007-09-29.abstract: President Bush said Friday that major carbon-emitting nations should all set goals for reducing greenhouse-gas emissions, but did not specify what those goals should be.

2007-09-29.title: Blacklisted Bank to Return to Original Owner
2007-09-29.abstract: A small family-owned bank in Macao accused by the United States of laundering money for North Korea will be returned to its original owner, Stanley Au.

2007-09-29.title: Mexico: Laws to Rein In Negative Campaigns Gain
2007-09-29.abstract: Constitutional amendments that would limit negative campaigns and profoundly change the rules for Mexican elections were assured of passage late Thursday when a majority of state legislatures ratified them.

2007-09-30.title: U.N. Envoy Brings Appeal for Restraint to Myanmar
2007-09-30.abstract: A special envoy arrived in Myanmar, carrying a message from a world that has watched with alarm the violent suppression of peaceful nationwide protests.

2007-09-30.title: Meeting Today’s Military Demands, With an Eye on Tomorrow’s
2007-09-30.abstract: When Adm. Mike Mullen is sworn in Monday as the new chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff, he will have to juggle both the daily demands of the war in Iraq and planning for future threats.

2007-09-30.title: Lawyers Battle Police Over Election Ruling in Pakistan
2007-09-30.abstract: The lawyers were protesting a Supreme Court ruling on Friday that cleared the way for President Pervez Musharraf to run for re-election while still in uniform.

2007-09-30.title: Bomber Attacks Bus of Afghan Soldiers; 30 Dead
2007-09-30.abstract: In one of the deadliest attacks in Afghanistan this year, a bomber wearing an Afghan military uniform approached a bus full of Afghan soldiers and detonated a belt of explosives.

2007-09-30.title: Under Iron Hand of Russia’s Proxy, a Chechen Revival
2007-09-30.abstract: Grozny, the capital, has reappeared from the rubble as the republic has fallen almost fully under the control of the Kremlin.
